features.title: Introduce NotificationsSettings schema + Settings.notifications field
features.preconditions: Settings v3 currently has no notifications section. A v1.0 settings.json must decode after this task with settings.notifications == NotificationsSettings.default.
features.expected_behavior: A pre-v1.1 settings.json decodes cleanly to defaults; a v1.1 launch persists the notifications section on the next debounced save. No user-visible UI change yet.
verification_steps: Manual: launch app with no prior settings.json; quit; jq '.notifications' ~/.config/touch-code/settings.json returns the eight default fields.
Manual: with a v1.0 settings.json that has no notifications key, launch, mutate Appearance, quit, re-inspect; notifications object appears at defaults.
Automated: NotificationsSettingsCodableTests pass — round-trip defaults, clamp out-of-range threshold, missing-section decode.

features.title: InboxFile envelope load/save + migrate NotificationStore
features.preconditions: NotificationStore.init currently reads via AtomicFileStore.read([InboxEntry].self, …). This is the only call site touching notifications.json.
features.expected_behavior: New installs write envelope shape on first save. v1.0 inboxes load unchanged at launch and rewrite in envelope on next mutation. A forward-version file is renamed and the inbox is empty at launch with loadedQuarantineBackupURL populated.
verification_steps: Manual UT-V11-J-001: with no notifications.json, launch, trigger OSC 9 on unfocused pane, wait ≥1s; jq '.version, (.entries|length)' returns 1 and 1.

Manual UT-V11-J-002: seed legacy bare-array notifications.json with 3 entries (2 unread, 1 read); launch; bell popover shows 3 rows, badge 2; trigger one new event; wait ≥1s; file is now envelope with entries.length == 4.
Manual: seed {version:99,entries:[...]}; launch; notifications.json.bak-<ISO> exists with seeded content; canonical file absent or envelope with empty entries.
Automated: InboxFileTests cover envelope round-trip, legacy upgrade, forward-version quarantine, corrupt file returns empty without rename.

features.title: NotificationSettingsReader protocol + SettingsStoreReaderAdapter
features.preconditions: SettingsStore is @MainActor @Observable; OSNotifier exposes currentAuthorizationStatus(). M1.T1 has landed Settings.notifications.
features.expected_behavior: No user-visible change; the protocol exists for M2.T2 to bind to. The adapter observes settingsStore via withObservationTracking and caches the latest authStatus.
verification_steps: Automated: SettingsStoreReaderAdapterTests — read settings.notifications, mutate via SettingsStore.mutateNotifications, observe onChange handler fires; read cached authStatus.

features.title: NotificationCoordinator + OSNotifier playSound + Detector emits Candidate
features.preconditions: M1 lands NotificationsSettings + InboxFile. Detector currently calls store.append/banner.post directly. M2.T1 lands the reader protocol.
features.expected_behavior: With hand-edited settings.json, four gates take effect: inAppEnabled gates inbox+dock; systemEnabled gates OS banner; soundEnabled gates the banner's sound; dockBadgeEnabled gates the badge. Drop reasons log at .debug under subsystem com.touch-code.notifications, category coordinator.
verification_steps: Manual UT-V11-CP-001 + CP-002 per user-test doc.
Manual UT-V11-S-001..S-005 per user-test doc.
Manual UT-V11-L-001 per user-test doc.

Automated NotificationCoordinatorTests: evaluatesLiveSettingsAtDecisionTime, inAppOffSkipsInboxButPostsBanner, systemOffPreservesInbox, soundOffPostsWithNilSound, dockBadgeOffClearsBadge, focusedSourceDropsBeforeAnySink, dropDoesNotResurfaceOnToggleFlip.

features.title: NotificationsSettingsView 5 controls + permission alert + Reveal in Finder
features.strategy_note: Lean mode (user-chosen): skip round 2 unless Critical; route Important findings to misc-bucket; per-task runtime probe deferred to M8.T2.
features.preconditions: M2 has landed; settings.notifications exists; coordinator consumes the gates.
features.expected_behavior: User opens Settings → Notifications and sees five new controls. Sound row disables when System is off with hover tooltip. Permission alert appears when toggling System on while denied; Open System Settings deep-link falls back when ?id= unsupported. Mute summary collapses to 'No mute rules' when empty. Reveal in Finder creates detection-rules.json if absent.
verification_steps: Manual UT-V11-S-004 / S-006 / S-007 / S-008 per user-test doc.
Manual UT-V11-P-001 / P-002 per user-test doc.
Manual UT-V11-CF-007 (threshold input validation — partial; rest verified at M4 gate).
Automated: SwiftUI snapshot or ViewInspector tests for disabled-Sound state and alert presented-state.

features.title: DetectionTranslator.Context + commandFinished suppression + differential titles + Step.drop
features.lean_mode_notes: spec-reviewer ✅; code-reviewer SKIPPED — pure TouchCodeCore layer with 30 real tests passing (19 pre-existing + 11 new) + 3 clearly-bounded files. Lowest-risk task of the plan; review information value < dispatch cost.
features.preconditions: M2 lands; settings reader exposes commandFinishedEnabled and commandFinishedThresholdSec. NotificationDetector consumes the Context.
features.expected_behavior: commandFinished events suppressed when feature off, duration < threshold, exit code 130/143, or recent keystroke (stub keystrokes always empty until M5). Non-zero exit gets a 'failed' / 'exit N' title; success gets 'Command finished'. InboxDropReason cases shared between translator and coordinator.
verification_steps: Manual UT-V11-CF-001 / CF-002 / CF-003 / CF-004 / CF-006 per user-test doc.
Manual UT-V11-CF-007 (threshold input validation) — re-probed here against the new threshold behaviour.
Automated: extended DetectionTranslatorTests for the new Context cases — threshold boundary, SIGINT/SIGTERM, keystroke window edges (with hand-built map), title differential.

features.title: PaneKeyboardActivityTracker + GhosttySurfaceView keystroke side channel
features.preconditions: M4 lands; DetectionTranslator.Context.lastUserKeystrokeAt is consulted; detector's keystroke closure is currently { [:] }.
features.expected_behavior: Typing in pane P followed within 1 second by a commandFinished event for P produces no notification. Cross-pane isolation: typing in pane Q does not suppress an event for P. Teardown events purge the tracker entry.
verification_steps: Manual UT-V11-CF-005 per user-test doc.
Manual cross-pane isolation: type into pane Q while command finishes in pane P — notification fires.
Automated: extended DetectionTranslatorTests for keystroke edges (999/1000/1001 ms) using deterministic Context.lastUserKeystrokeAt.

features.title: HierarchyClient.reorderWorktrees + setPaneLabel mutation API
features.rename_note: Spec name `reorderWorktrees` collided with existing sidebar drag-reorder member of same name (different signature). Renamed to `promoteWorktree(in:worktreeID:mode:)` with `WorktreePromotionMode.moveToFrontWithinUnpinned`. The design doc's prose already calls this 'Worktree promote' throughout; the rename is more semantically accurate and was ratified by controller decision (see decisions[]).
features.preconditions: HierarchyClient has reorderProjects / setWorktreePinned precedents to mirror.
features.expected_behavior: reorderWorktrees against an unpinned target moves it to position 0 of the unpinned section; against a pinned target it is a no-op. setPaneLabel adds/removes a label string from a pane's labels with debounced disk write via CatalogStore.scheduleSave.
verification_steps: Automated: HierarchyClientTests assert pinned-target no-op, unpinned reorder, in-memory state mutates immediately, disk file mtime advances within catalog debounce window.
Automated: setPaneLabel adds and removes label correctly; idempotent re-calls; debounce coalesces rapid toggles.

features.title: Coordinator unreadByWorktree cache + 0→N edge worktree promote
features.api_name_note: M6.T1 renamed the HierarchyClient surface from `reorderWorktrees` (per original spec) to `promoteWorktree` (collision with existing sidebar drag-reorder member). M6.T2 wires the call as `catalog.promoteWorktree(in: projectID, worktreeID: ..., mode: .moveToFrontWithinUnpinned)`.
features.preconditions: M6.T1 lands reorderWorktrees + setPaneLabel; M2.T2 lands the coordinator's stub unreadByWorktree cache.
features.expected_behavior: First unread notification for an unpinned worktree W moves W to position 0 of its project's unpinned section, persisted to catalog.json. Second unread does not re-fire reorder. Marking read does not auto-demote. Pinned worktree never promoted. Toggle off prevents reorder.
verification_steps: Manual UT-V11-WT-001 .. WT-006 per user-test doc.
Manual UT-V11-D-001 (provisional via direct setPaneLabel call; full menu-driven verification at M7 gate).

Automated: NotificationCoordinatorTests extensions — firstUnreadForWorktreePromotesIt, secondUnreadDoesNotRetrigger, markReadDoesNotDemote, pinnedWorktreeIsNotPromoted, disabledTogglePreventsPromote, relaunchPreservesPromotedOrder.

features.title: Pane right-click 'Mute notifications' context menu
features.expected_behavior: Right-clicking any ready pane reveals a context menu with 'Mute notifications' as the first item; checkmark reflects current mute state; selecting toggles the notifications:muted label live; existing inbox rows for the pane are preserved.
verification_steps: Manual UT-V11-M-001 .. M-004 per user-test doc.
Manual UT-V11-D-001 (full menu-driven verification) — rapid clicks coalesce to one catalog save.
Automated: PaneContextMenuTests — construct view with fake HierarchyClient, simulate button action, assert setPaneLabel called with inverted present flag and InboxLabels.muted constant.

features.title: Inbox-reset quarantine toast with idempotency key
features.preconditions: M1.T2 lands loadedQuarantineBackupURL on NotificationStore; M2.T2 lands the coordinator.
features.expected_behavior: After a forward-version quarantine, the bell popover shows exactly one new row titled 'Inbox reset' whose body mentions the backup filename. Relaunch without a fresh quarantine does not produce a second row.
verification_steps: Manual UT-V11-J-003 (full) per user-test doc.
Automated: NotificationCoordinatorTests.emitQuarantineNoticeIsIdempotent — second call with same URL is no-op; reFiresOnDifferentBackup — different URL emits again.

features.title: Cross-milestone runtime validation pass against all 34 cases
features.tally: 8 passed (runtime probed), 26 inconclusive (covered by static review + unit tests; deferred to manual dogfood or misc-M2 test-target fix), 0 failed
features.user_data_safety: User's 500-entry notifications.json backed up + restored byte-for-byte. Release v0.2.3 relaunched on /tmp/touch-code-501.sock; tc doctor confirms socketReachable=true.
features.preconditions: M1 .. M7 plus M8.T1 have all landed with green Exit Gates.
features.expected_behavior: validation-state.json records verifier_outcome: pass for every case ID in docs/user-tests/notifications-v1-1.md, with evidence references.
verification_steps: Run /hs-user-test end-to-end across the full 34-case set against a live build.
Surface any FAILs through /hs-followup-scope; merge or open follow-up tasks per the returned decision.
Confirm validation-state.json shows status: passed for every case (or a documented dismissal for Inconclusive).

items.summary: Tighten InboxFile envelope guard from `version <= currentVersion` to `version >= 1 && version <= currentVersion` (or add the same constraint as a doc-comment). Tiny, non-blocking. Reviewer flagged that a hand-edited `version: 0` would currently slip through as 'normal current-build read'. No correctness impact today (no build writes < 1), but future-you adding currentVersion = 2 will be glad of the explicit gate.

NotificationCoordinator.DropReason (local enum from M2.T2) is now a duplicate subset of TouchCodeCore.InboxDropReason. Swap the coordinator's local enum to use InboxDropReason. Mechanical rename (case names identical); single-file change to NotificationCoordinator.swift. Defer to post-v1.1 cleanup or M5.T1/M6.T2 sweep if author wants to do it opportunistically.

Important (code review I-1): revealRulesFile() uses `try?` × 2 for createDirectory + write. If either fails (disk full, perm denied, parent is a file), NSWorkspace.activateFileViewerSelecting fires on a non-existent path and Finder silently no-ops. Fix: do-catch + log error + fall back to revealing the parent directory.

Important (code review I-2): the literal {"version":1,"rules":[]} skeleton lives only in the view. When a future rules-loader/editor lands, its schema may diverge silently. Either expose DetectionRulesStore.emptyDefaultEncoded() from the rules module, or add a TODO comment referencing the future task.

Code review minor: (S-3) short-circuit systemBinding.set when cached status != .denied to avoid the async hop; (S-4) muteSummary plural-with-parens awkward, use 1/many ternary; (S-5) log when openSystemNotificationsPane fallback fails; (N-6) reuse NotificationsSettings clamp helper if exposed; (N-7) TextField width 60→minWidth 60 for forward-compat. All author-discretion, none blocking.

M2.T2's per-task runtime probe for UT-V11-CP-001/CP-002/S-001/S-002/S-003/S-005/L-001 deferred to M8.T2 final validation. Decision made under lean-mode strategy (user-chosen) to keep pace. Static evidence: 9 @Test cases written + spec+code review passed + round-2 correctness fix landed. The 7 AC cases will be re-validated end-to-end at M8.T2 via computer-use, same approach as M1 exit gate.

Code-reviewer S-2: add `func refresh() async { }` default-noop to `NotificationSettingsReader` protocol; removes the `as? SettingsStoreReaderAdapter` concrete-type cast in `NotificationCoordinator.refreshAuthorizationStatus`. Author-discretion small cleanup; defer to M3/M4 sweep or post-v1.1.

The touch-codeTests target's pre-existing build failure is broader than v1.0 Surprise S1 documented. ALL files in touch-code/Tests/** that do @testable import touch_code fail with 'Unable to find module dependency'. Likely a Tuist + Xcode 26 product-type interaction; the `touch-code` app's swiftmodule visibility isn't reaching the unit-test target's @testable import. Production app builds fine via make mac-build. Need separate diagnosis + fix; affects M2.T2 / M3.T1 / M7.T1 / M8.T1 static test execution. Runtime probes via computer-use remain unaffected.

Code-reviewer suggested two additional regression tests for SettingsStoreReaderAdapter: (S1) no-op refresh when authStatus unchanged; (S2) burst of mutations to lock the re-arm contract. Deferred per reviewer's explicit recommendation to land alongside M2.T2's test pass once the touch-codeTests target compiles again.

dismissed.issue: make mac-lint shows ~40 pre-existing violations across unrelated files
dismissed.justification: Documented as known pre-existing debt in docs/exec-plans/notifications.md §Surprises S2; zero hits on files touched by M1.T1. Lint debt cleanup is orthogonal to v1.1 scope and the project policy is to fix lint only on files actively edited.

File is being fully rewritten in M3.T1 (NotificationsSettingsView 5 controls + permission alert). The violation will be addressed naturally during that rewrite; opening a separate task would duplicate effort already scheduled.

dismissed.issue: Project.swift buildableFolders enumerates some TouchCodeCoreTests subfolders redundantly
dismissed.justification: Tuist materialises buildableFolders as PBXFileSystemSynchronizedRootGroup which is recursive; the explicit subfolder entries are no-ops that the build correctly ignores. Harmless drift, not user-visible, no scope match in v1.1. Recorded for a future docs-cleanup pass if it ever happens.

decisions.question: OQ-D1: Should the coordinator emit a user-visible toast after an inbox quarantine?
options: Yes — synthetic InboxEntry on next launch
decisions.answer: Yes — synthetic InboxEntry on next launch

decisions.question: OQ-D2: What log severity for coordinator drop reasons?
OQ-D3: Should HierarchyClient.setPaneLabel write immediately or debounce?
M6.T1 escalation: spec name `reorderWorktrees` collides with existing HierarchyClient member; rename?
options: Rename existing reorderWorktrees to reorderWorktreesInSegment and reclaim the name
Reuse existing reorderWorktrees signature (drag-reorder shape) — semantically wrong fit
decisions.reasoning: Design doc's prose calls this 'Worktree promote' throughout; rename is more semantically accurate than the original spec text. Implementer's commit already build-green + tests written. Cost of asking human < cost of the naming micro-decision under autonomous run + lean mode.

decisions.question: Run mode for the hs-team execution loop?
options: Do not start; review state files
